examples.title: Pseudocode: Simple Servlet
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic structure of a simple servlet
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SimpleServlet
INPUT: HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse
OUTPUT: HTML Response

BEGIN
 // Handle GET request
 OVERRIDE doGet(HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse)
 SET response content type to text/html
 WRITE HTML response using PrintWriter
 CLOSE PrintWriter
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the basic structure of a simple servlet, handling GET requests and sending HTML responses.

examples.title: Java: Simple Servlet
examples.description: A basic Java servlet that handles GET requests and sends HTML responses
examples.explanation: This Java servlet handles GET requests and sends HTML responses using the PrintWriter.

examples.title: Java: Annotated Servlet
examples.description: A Java servlet that uses annotations to map the servlet to a URL
examples.explanation: This Java servlet uses the @WebServlet annotation to map the servlet to a URL.
